expectations.evidence: The without_skill run was instructed not to read the reference document. The review uses WCAG-style numbering (e.g., '1.1.1', '2.4.4', '4.1.2', '4.2.1') rather than the KWCAG2.2 sequential 1-33 item numbering defined in kwcag22.md. This confirms the reference was not consulted.

expectations.text: Analyzes all 3 target files (index.html, dashboard.tsx, Card.tsx)
expectations.evidence: The review has dedicated sections for all 3 files: '1. index.html 접근성 검토', '2. dashboard.tsx 접근성 검토', '3. Card.tsx 접근성 검토'. Specific line references are provided for each file.

expectations.text: Evaluates all 33 KWCAG2.2 items — none skipped
expectations.evidence: The review does not systematically evaluate all 33 KWCAG2.2 items. It identifies 12 specific issues across 3 files organized by severity (4 심각, 6 경고, 2 정보) and maps them to WCAG criteria, but does not enumerate or evaluate items like captions, auto-play, character key shortcuts, pointer cancellation, etc. The KWCAG2.2 원칙별 위반 요약 table only lists violations found, not all 33 items.

Uses a severity-based system instead: [심각], [경고], [정보]. Does not use the KWCAG classification symbols (✅/❌/⚠️/➖/🔵) or (O/X/△/N/A). The summary table uses severity counts (심각 4, 경고 6, 정보 2) rather than compliance classification.

There is no dedicated semantic HTML review section. While some semantic HTML issues are mentioned in passing (e.g., section 1.4 notes missing landmark elements), there is no structured evaluation of semantic HTML patterns like presentational tags, line breaks, inline wrapping, landmarks, section structure, etc.

expectations.text: Each ❌ finding includes filename and line reference
expectations.evidence: All findings include filename and line references. Examples: 'index.html 30행', 'dashboard.tsx 29~31행', 'Card.tsx 24~33행', 'index.html 37~44행'. The KWCAG2.2 원칙별 위반 요약 table also includes file references for each violation.
